names.thesis: Rusal remains loss-making and has not paid dividends since H1 2022, with no plans to resume for Q1 2026, at a time when LME aluminum has fallen ~15.5% in June to ~$3,112 and buyers are told to purchase only as needed. Simply Wall St's DCF puts fair value near HK$0.32 versus a HK$3.1 share price, implying the stock is trading well above intrinsic cash-flow value even as the company keeps investing capex in digital-twin refinery upgrades rather than returning cash.
catalysts: Any recovery in alumina/aluminum prices
Digital twin rollout across alumina refineries improving unit costs by 2028
risks: Western sanctions on Russian entities make this effectively untradeable/high-risk for a US-based book
No dividend visibility while metallurgy sector-wide (NLMK, MMK) also suspends payouts
Aluminum backdrop is SHORT into a falling LME market
Foreign listing (HKEX) with limited IBKR tradability given sanctions exposure

evidence.note: Rusal affiliates awarded only $71M in Ukraine plant dispute vs. $1B+ sought
Rusal has not paid dividends since H1 2022 and will not for Q1 2026; Russian metallurgy broadly suspending payouts
Rusal deploying 100+ digital twins across alumina refineries by 2028 to improve process efficiency
DCF fair value ~HK$0.32 vs HK$3.1 trading price; company currently loss-making with earnings under pressure

names.thesis: South32 agreed to sell its bauxite/alumina/aluminum operations to Alcoa for ~$4.1B upfront cash-and-stock plus a CVR worth up to $750M tied to future alumina/aluminum prices, exiting a segment squarely in the SHORT aluminum backdrop and crystallizing value at a premium; separately South32 is set to gain US Forest Service approval for its $2B Arizona zinc-manganese mine (Hermosa) with full production targeted by 2029, shifting the remaining portfolio toward the LONG zinc trade. Divestiture proceeds plus a growth project in a bullish commodity is a favorable re-rating catalyst, though close isn't until H1 2027.
catalysts: Alcoa deal shareholder/regulatory approval and close (H1 2027)
US Forest Service approval for Hermosa Arizona zinc-manganese mine
CVR payments if alumina/aluminum prices exceed strike prices
risks: Long ~1-year wait to closing introduces deal risk
CVR payout is contingent on higher alumina/aluminum prices, which are currently under pressure
ASX/LSE/JSE multi-listing complicates IBKR tradability for US accounts

evidence.note: Alcoa to acquire South32 bauxite/alumina/aluminum assets for $4.1B cash+stock, ~$900M NPV synergies expected
South32 set to gain US approval for $2B Arizona zinc/manganese mine, full production targeted 2029
Up to $750M CVR tied to alumina/aluminum prices exceeding strike prices over four annual periods; close expected H1 2027
Deal expected to lift Alcoa production capacity to 3.2Mt aluminum/14.8Mt alumina, EPS +85% YoY to $8.09 in 2026

names.thesis: Tata Steel posted FY2025-26 consolidated EBITDA of INR34,848 crore (+35% YoY) with margins expanding 320bps to 15%, record deliveries, INR10,738 crore free cash flow, and dual investment-grade ratings, while commissioning Kalinganagar Phase II to reach 26.1 MTPA capacity en route to a 40 MTPA long-term target — consistent with the LONG steel backdrop. The main offsetting risk is a Competition Commission of India probe into 32 steel producers (including Tata Steel) over alleged HRC/CRC/TMT price coordination, which the company disputes but which adds regulatory overhang.
catalysts: Continued Kalinganagar and NINL capacity ramp-up toward 40 MTPA
Value-added/downstream (Tubes, Tinplate, Wires) mix shift supporting margins
US HRC price tightness (tariff-protected) supporting realizations
risks: CCI antitrust investigation into HRC/CRC/TMT pricing across 32 Indian steelmakers
China steel oversupply/weak demand capping global price ceiling
NSE-listed; foreign-ordinary-share access via IBKR may be limited, GDR/ADR alternative should be checked

evidence.note: Kalinganagar Phase II commissioned, capacity to 26.1 MTPA, path to 40 MTPA; INR4/share FY26 dividend recommended

evidence.source: Model ML - Regulatory Filings (Tata Steel Annual Report)
evidence.note: FY26 consolidated EBITDA +35% YoY to INR34,848cr, margin 15% (+320bps); CCI investigating 32 steel producers incl. Tata Steel over HRC/CRC/TMT pricing

names.thesis: Berenberg reiterated Buy and raised its Glencore price target twice recently (to £7.80 from £6.30) citing a 'copper-plated bridge to the future' with copper output guided to roughly double from ~800ktpa to 1.6mtpa by 2035, funded by resilient Marketing/coal cash flow and a strong balance sheet — directly aligned with the LONG copper backdrop. RBC also prefers Glencore among diversified miners for its lack of iron-ore exposure and potential portfolio restructuring (coal spin-off, copper stake sales), with H1 2026 production/results due July 29 and Aug 5.
catalysts: H1 2026 production report (Jul 29) and results (Aug 5)
Progress on ~1.4Mt of incremental long-life copper production through approval phases
Potential coal spin-off / portfolio restructuring unlocking value
risks: Steelmaking coal/energy segment still a earnings drag amid weak coal pricing
Execution risk on multi-decade copper production ramp
OTC (GLNCY) ADR liquidity thinner than LSE-listed GLEN; verify IBKR execution quality

evidence.note: RBC prefers Glencore among diversified miners; H1 EBITDA seen at $9.8B, restructuring optionality (coal spin, copper stake sales)
Record Metals & Minerals performance, copper volumes/prices recovering H2; 2026 priorities include disciplined cost management and copper growth
Berenberg reiterated Buy, PT £7.80, citing copper-centred high-growth strategy funded by legacy cash generation
Berenberg raised PT to £7.80 from £6.30 ahead of Aug 5 H1 results; copper output guided to 1.6mtpa by 2035

names.thesis: No recent Bigdata.com coverage was retrievable for Hitachi across two search attempts (entity resolution returned zero matched chunks both times), so no name-specific evidence is available. Positioning defaults to the steel commodity backdrop (LONG, tariff-tight US HRC but a softening China market), applied cautiously since Hitachi's electrical-steel exposure is a small, indirect slice of a large diversified industrial conglomerate rather than a pure-play producer.
catalysts: Electrification/grid-transformer demand supporting electrical steel demand (unconfirmed for this name specifically)
risks: No verifiable recent news flow found — thesis is backdrop-only, not company-specific
Tokyo Stock Exchange listing; confirm IBKR tradability/ADR availability before sizing

evidence.note: No recent Bigdata coverage returned for Hitachi across two query attempts

names.thesis: Agnico disclosed a rock mass movement at the Barnat open pit (Canadian Malartic complex) that will cut full-year 2026 gold production by 60,000-80,000oz in H2 (guidance now expected near the low end of 3.3-3.5Moz) with further 2027-2028 impacts of up to ~150,000oz/year, even though Q2 production of ~845,000oz was slightly ahead of plan; shares are already down 5.5% over the past month, underperforming both the S&P 500 and Basic Materials sector. As one of the lower-cost, longer-life major producers, AEM fits the 'low-cost defensive' pocket of the gold SHORT-medium backdrop, but the near-term guidance cut and softer gold tape argue for a neutral rather than outright bullish stance ahead of the July 29 Q2 report.
catalysts: Q2 2026 results and updated production/cost guidance (Jul 29)
Resolution/mitigation plan for Barnat geotechnical issue
Canadian Malartic complex progressing toward 1Moz/year in early 2030s
risks: Multi-year production impact (2026-2028) from Barnat rock mass movement
Gold backdrop is SHORT-medium on hawkish Fed, -14% 3M
Geotechnical/seismic risk also present at LaRonde, Goldex, Fosterville

evidence.note: Barnat rock mass movement cuts H2 2026 production 60-80koz gold; FY26 guidance now near low end of 3.3-3.5Moz range
AEM shares down 5.53% over past month, underperforming Basic Materials sector; Q2 earnings due Jul 29, consensus EPS $3.14

names.thesis: Kinross delivered a 4th consecutive quarter of record free cash flow ($838M), is on track for 2026 production guidance (~2Moz/yr), added $440M to the balance sheet, and is targeting 40% of FCF returned to shareholders (~$350M YTD) with $1.4B net cash and a ~12% FCF yield — genuinely strong low-cost operational execution (Paracatu, Tasiast). However, BofA just cut its price target sharply to $32.75 from $46 (while keeping a Buy), signaling analysts are pricing in a weaker gold-price path consistent with the SHORT-medium gold backdrop, which offsets the operating strength.
catalysts: Great Bear and Lobo-Marte greenfield project milestones
Continued FCF-funded buybacks/dividend growth (33% dividend increase since Q3/25)
Q2 2026 results confirming guidance
risks: Large recent sell-side price target cut (BofA -29%) despite maintained Buy rating
Gold backdrop SHORT-medium on hawkish Fed and -14% 3M move
Cost/production guidance still pending formal Q2 update

evidence.note: Record FCF $838M in Q1, on track for 2026 guidance, $1.4B net cash, targeting 40% of FCF returned to shareholders
BofA Securities cut Kinross price target to $32.75 from $46 while maintaining Buy rating

names.thesis: Cobalt Blue and Glomar Minerals have narrowed their proposed US polymetallic-nodule refinery (Project Infinity, targeting manganese, cobalt, nickel and copper) from 30+ candidate sites to a shortlist of 4 in Texas, North Carolina and Louisiana, with new ocean surveys planned July-October 2026 and feasibility work to begin once funding is secured — a credible but very early-stage, pre-revenue critical-minerals optionality story rather than a producing manganese exposure today. Given manganese's NEUTRAL commodity backdrop and the multi-year path to a resource statement/funding/construction, this is a speculative call-option position, not a near-term earnings driver.
catalysts: Maiden resource statement from Glomar's CCZ licences
Site selection finalization and techno-economic evaluation
Funding secured to begin detailed feasibility studies
risks: Pre-revenue, pre-feasibility stage with no committed financing for the refinery
Deep-sea nodule mining carries regulatory/environmental permitting uncertainty
ASX-listed micro-cap; confirm IBKR/OTC (CBBHF) tradability and liquidity

evidence.note: Project Infinity site shortlist narrowed to 4 US locations; targets manganese, cobalt, nickel, copper from polymetallic nodules
Facility would produce refined manganese, cobalt sulfate, nickel and copper metal; sites moving to techno-economic evaluation

names.thesis: Manganese X is a pre-production TSXV junior advancing its Battery Hill manganese-carbonate deposit toward a Pre-Feasibility Study (targeted fall 2026), recently strengthened by ~C$1.71M from noted mining investor Eric Sprott exercising warrants (now ~35% owner) and by promising Phase 3 battery-qualification testing with C4V showing 70% capacity retention after 4,600 cycles. This is credible junior-development progress and validation, but with no production, revenue, or offtake yet, it remains a speculative, binary story against a NEUTRAL manganese backdrop.
catalysts: Battery Hill PFS completion (targeted fall 2026)
Successful Phase 3 C4V battery qualification supporting offtake discussions
Further Sprott or strategic investor support
risks: Pre-revenue junior with financing/dilution risk ahead of PFS and beyond
No offtake agreements secured yet
TSXV micro-cap; confirm OTCQB (MNXXF) liquidity via IBKR

evidence.note: C$1.71M raised via Sprott warrant exercise (now ~35% owner); Battery Hill PFS targeted fall 2026; Phase 3 C4V testing underway
Confirms Sprott financing and PFS timeline for Battery Hill manganese project

names.thesis: KGHM's new 'Strategy 2055+' commits over PLN32B ($8B) of investment through 2030 targeting a 25.6% average EBITDA margin, a new domestic KGHM 2.0 mine, and continued growth in Chile, the US and Canada, while April 2026 operating data already show copper sales +4% YoY and molybdenum sales +100% YoY (0.4M lbs) — direct leverage to both the LONG copper backdrop (LME $13,357, up-trend) and improving molybdenum by-product economics. The company also resumed a modest dividend (PLN1.50/share for 2025), evidencing balance-sheet capacity to fund growth capex.
catalysts: Molybdenum by-product volume growth continuing to scale
Progress on KGHM 2.0 and Chile/US/Canada project pipeline
Copper price up-trend supporting the core Polish/Sierra Gorda operations
risks: Large multi-year capex programme (PLN32B) carries execution and funding risk
Silver by-product exposure is a headwind given SHORT-low silver backdrop
Warsaw Stock Exchange (GPW) listing; verify IBKR access/ADR for KGH.WA

evidence.note: PLN32B+ investment programme through 2030, 25.6% avg EBITDA margin target, new KGHM 2.0 mine planned
April 2026: copper sales +4% YoY to 56.1kt; molybdenum sales +100% YoY to 0.4M lbs
AGM approved PLN1.50/share 2025 dividend

names.thesis: IGO's fortunes now rest almost entirely on its Greenbushes lithium joint venture rather than nickel — legacy nickel assets are winding down — and Kalkine Media reports 'systemic operational issues' with trimmed output expectations even as sharply higher spodumene prices lift revenue and offset a higher cost outlook; a June fire at the CGP3 plant (extinguished, no injuries) did not change FY26 spodumene guidance of 1.4Mt, but an interim CFO appointment adds leadership-transition risk. Because the company's real commodity exposure has shifted to lithium (not covered by this backdrop) rather than nickel (NEUTRAL-bearish, stainless off-season), this is a mismatched/idiosyncratic story best treated as neutral pending clarity on the Greenbushes turnaround.
catalysts: Resolution of Greenbushes 'systemic operational issues' and cost trajectory
Permanent CFO appointment
Continued lithium price recovery supporting revenue
risks: Operational/turnaround uncertainty at Greenbushes flagged explicitly by press
Interim CFO signals management transition risk
Chinese lithium supply (restarted suspended operation) could cap price upside
ASX-listed; confirm IBKR tradability/liquidity for IGO.AX

evidence.note: IGO trimmed Greenbushes output expectations amid 'systemic operational issues'; nickel legacy assets winding down, fortunes now tied to lithium JV
Fire at CGP3 lithium plant extinguished without injury; FY26 spodumene guidance of 1.4Mt maintained
Higher spodumene pricing offsetting increased operating costs; Chinese lithium operation restart a supply-side risk
IGO appointed interim CFO Ian Rowe; shares fell 4% on the news

names.thesis: As China's dominant light rare earth (Baotou) producer, Northern Rare Earth posted Q1 2026 revenue of RMB11.86B, up 27.69% YoY, and set its Q3 2026 rare earth concentrate transaction price at RMB38,565/tonne — a modest 0.62% sequential decline from Q2's RMB38,800/tonne but still reflecting the structurally elevated pricing consistent with the LONG rare earths backdrop (NdPr +74% YoY, supply gap). The company's quasi-monopoly position on China's concentrate pricing mechanism gives it direct, near-monopolistic leverage to the rare earth price complex.
catalysts: Continued NdPr/rare earth oxide price strength feeding through to quarterly concentrate pricing resets
Chinese export-quota policy tightening supply further
Downstream new-material and magnet business growth
risks: Sequential (QoQ) concentrate price softness (-0.62% in Q3) bears watching for trend reversal
State-directed pricing mechanism reduces pure market-price leverage
Shanghai A-share listing; confirm IBKR/QFII access — foreign tradability is limited without Stock Connect/appropriate account

evidence.note: Q1 2026 operating revenue RMB11.859B, +27.69% YoY; Q3 concentrate price mechanism confirmed
Q3 2026 rare earth concentrate price set at RMB38,565/tonne
Q3 concentrate price down 0.62% QoQ from RMB38,800/tonne in Q2

names.thesis: Neo Performance Materials raised full-year 2026 Adjusted EBITDA guidance to $140-150M from $100-110M (+38% at the midpoint, ~90% above FY2025's $76M), explicitly citing sustained higher-than-expected pricing for hafnium, gallium and rare earth elements amid tight global supply and growing supply-chain-security demand — a textbook direct confirmation of the LONG rare earths backdrop. Management notes secured hafnium/gallium/tantalum inventory and increased contracted volumes giving added confidence into H2 2026, with Q2 results due August 11.
catalysts: Q2 2026 results (Aug 11) testing the raised guidance
New Permanent Magnet facility in Europe commercial launch
Continued elevated critical-materials pricing (hafnium, gallium, REE)
risks: Guidance raise already partly priced in after the announcement
Exposure to China export-control/tariff policy on rare earth inputs
Magnequench/automotive customer concentration risk

evidence.note: FY2026 Adjusted EBITDA guidance raised to $140-150M from $100-110M on elevated hafnium/gallium/REE pricing and tight supply
Confirms 38% midpoint guidance increase driven by strong operating performance and pricing

names.thesis: Howmet's Q1 2026 results beat expectations across the board — revenue +19% YoY to $2.31B, adjusted EPS $1.22, operating income +52% to $753M, EBITDA +32% to $740M (32% margin), free cash flow more than doubled to $359M — driving raised full-year guidance to $9.65B revenue and $4.94 adjusted EPS, with 18 of 23 analysts rating the stock Strong Buy and an average price target implying ~12.5% upside. This is a demand-driven (aerospace/gas-turbine engine components) rather than raw-titanium-price-driven bull case, but it benefits from the same titanium supply-chain tightness underpinning the broader materials backdrop.
catalysts: Q2 2026 results and webcast (Aug 6)
Continued aerospace OEM production ramp (narrowbody/widebody)
Further guidance raises if commercial aerospace demand holds
risks: Valuation already reflects strong Strong Buy consensus and 50.9% 52-week outperformance
Not a pure commodity-price play — more exposed to aerospace build-rate cycles than titanium spot pricing
Aerospace supply chain disruption or OEM production rate cuts

evidence.note: Q2 2026 results/webcast scheduled Aug 6
Q1 2026 revenue +19% YoY to $2.31B, adj EPS $1.22 beat; FY26 guidance raised to $9.65B revenue, $4.94 adj EPS
Deutsche Bank raised Howmet price target to $338 from $320; average analyst PT $311.37, overweight rating

names.thesis: Boliden's Garpenberg mine remains disrupted by a March 2026 seismic event affecting the Lappberget orebody (~70% of near-term planned volumes), creating near-term earnings uncertainty, but Berenberg turned 'more constructive' on the recovery (PT raised to SEK670) citing better-than-expected medium-term volume guidance and Q2 output guided at 0.1mt, while AlphaValue/Baader separately raised its PT to SEK706, framing Boliden as a 'reliable' European base/precious-metals proxy with ~96% of sales from the region — consistent with the LONG zinc/tin backdrop and limited direct exposure to Middle East disruption. Multiple brokers see the recent sell-off as an attractive entry point rather than a structural impairment.
catalysts: Garpenberg rehabilitation progress and volume guidance updates
Continued strength in zinc/tin prices feeding through to realized margins
Byproduct precious-metals and sulphuric-acid exposure as an upside kicker
risks: Garpenberg orebody disruption could prove more persistent than currently modeled
EPS estimates were cut ~12% for 2026 even as PT was raised
Stockholm-listed; confirm IBKR access/liquidity for BOL.ST

evidence.note: AlphaValue/Baader raised PT to SEK706, calling Boliden a reliable European metals proxy amid critical-minerals supply chain focus
AlphaValue/Baader called recent sell-off an attractive entry point; cut 2026 EPS ~12% on Garpenberg but reiterated positive rating
Berenberg raised PT to SEK670, 'more constructive' on Garpenberg recovery, better-than-expected medium-term volume guidance

names.thesis: Moody's upgraded Volcan's ratings to B1 from B2 (outlook stable), citing improved cash-flow visibility from the June 2026 ramp-up of the Romina polymetallic project (expected to grow zinc and silver production 26% and 10% respectively over 2026-2028 at a lower ~$50/MT cost versus the 2025 consolidated $54.3/MT), $280M cash on hand, targeted $60M of 2026 cost reductions, and no major debt maturities before 2032; Fitch separately estimates a 10% rise in zinc/silver prices would add $65-80M to 2026 EBITDA, directly levering the LONG zinc backdrop. The company remains a top-10 global zinc producer, though scale, geographic concentration in Peru, and a third-quartile cost position are structural constraints.
catalysts: Romina ramp-up driving zinc/silver volume growth through 2028
Targeted $60M (2026) and $20M (2027) cost reductions toward $57/MT unit cost
Further credit rating upgrades on improved FCF
risks: NOT tradable via IBKR (sanctioned/foreign-exchange listing)
Single-country (Peru) concentration and modest scale versus global peers
Third-quartile cost position versus peer average
Lima-listed; foreign listing likely has very limited or no IBKR tradability — verify ADR/OTC alternative before considering exposure

evidence.note: Production expected to recover to ~280,000 MT in 2026; 10% zinc/silver price rise adds $65-80M to 2026 EBITDA
Romina ramp-up (June 2026) to cut unit costs to ~$50/MT; targeted $60M cost reduction in 2026
Moody's upgraded Volcan to B1 from B2, outlook stable, on improved cash flow visibility and $280M cash position
